intent.one_sentence: A mossy forest creek shrine with shallow water, rocks, wood, vegetation, and soft filtered light.
intent.art_goal: Prove a natural scene can be authored and cohesive without random vegetation scatter.

foreground_midground_background.foreground: Shallow creek stones and reflected leaves.
foreground_midground_background.midground: Grounded shrine, moss, and fallen wood.

lighting.key: Soft filtered green-gold sun through canopy.
lighting.rim: Warm low highlights on creek and shrine edges.
lighting.fog: Thin ground mist over creek and undergrowth.

cameras.role: Creek leading line into shrine and vegetation layers.
cameras.art_reason: Tighter creek/material crop prioritizes water, moss rocks, and foreground vegetation while the narrowed creek reduces broad flooded-plane and weak shrine blockout exposure.

cameras.role: Moss, wet rock, and shallow water detail.
cameras.art_reason: Checks wetness and material blend without losing contact.

cameras.role: Shrine grounding and background vegetation depth.
cameras.art_reason: Shows shrine base, rocks, and foliage layers.

material_palette.description: Stone with moss accumulation and damp edges.
Shallow water with visible stone underneath.
Soft green vegetation with light variation.

assets.art_reason: Grounded focal object; no floating shrine problem.
Water follows a narrower banked path instead of reading as a broad flooded plane.
A scaled-down trunk anchors the bank without cutting across the whole hero image.
A stump gives the creek bank a believable forest anchor instead of a cube wall.
Moss rocks add wet bank detail and reduce the repeated boulder look.
Smaller hero ferns frame the creek without swallowing the image as giant leaves.
Grounded bush scans replace the earlier floating primitive canopy blobs.

authored_groups.art_reason: Water is bounded by rocks and banks rather than floating flatly.
Shrine, plinth, moss, and stones are a grounded focal cluster.
Vegetation is clustered by bank and background layer, not uniform random scatter.

seeded_random_groups.art_reason: Small secondary litter follows creek banks only.
